note: Editorial maturity scores (0-3, higher = more mature) across 45 assessments. 0 = absent, 1 = legacy/hidden/rented, 2 = usable/partial, 3 = modern/owned/complete. First 15 hand-authored; the rest self-scored per domain. See SYNTHESIS.md.

dimensions.label: Platform modernity
dimensions.desc: How current the delivery stack is.

dimensions.label: Site / content API
dimensions.desc: Does the site expose a usable, public API of its own?

dimensions.label: Open Data breadth
dimensions.desc: How much of the agency's data is on data.cityofnewyork.us.

dimensions.label: Core citizen-data machine-readable
dimensions.desc: Are the things residents most need (permits, schools, bills, results) machine-readable at all?

dimensions.label: Transactional (write) API
dimensions.desc: Can a resident DO the key civic action via API?

dimensions.label: Vendor independence
dimensions.desc: Is the core capability owned rather than rented/locked to a vendor?

notes.platform: Smarty 2.6.2 / jQuery (2005-era), but one modern Next.js island (Tree Map).
notes.siteApi: Tree Map internal JSON API + self-hosted /search; no public API.
notes.openData: 237 DPR assets — the twins already exist.
notes.coreData: Facilities/parks/monuments/trees have twins; permits do not.
notes.writeApi: Permit applications are login-gated HTML, no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Search is self-hosted (not rented); minor vendors (Cloudinary, Loggly).

notes.platform: Progress Sitefinity (.NET) — modern-ish CMS.
notes.siteApi: Internal /CustomApi drives pages but is robots-blocked & undocumented.
notes.openData: 638 assets incl. 462-column directories — the broadest.
notes.coreData: Directories/demographics/results yes; enrollment no.
notes.writeApi: MySchools admissions has no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Discovery rented to HawkSearch; heavy vendor footprint.

notes.platform: WordPress on WP Engine — modern.
notes.siteApi: Open WordPress REST API exists (content, not resources); Legistar API too.
notes.openData: 11 NYCC datasets — decent but flattened snapshots.
notes.coreData: Legislation, votes, hearings all live in the Legistar API — the most machine-readable core.
notes.writeApi: Testimony sign-up has no API; constituent-services data exists.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: The legislative record depends on the Granicus/Legistar vendor API.

notes.platform: Drupal 9, but JSON:API disabled.
notes.siteApi: None — no content or data API.
notes.openData: 2 assets (poll sites only).
notes.coreData: Results, candidates, contests are PDFs + a legacy viewer.
notes.writeApi: Ballot request is a siloed app, no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Mostly first-party; few vendors — but little to own yet.

notes.platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 — modern, but a proprietary vendor CRM.
notes.siteApi: api.nyc.gov gateway exists but GeoClient-only & key-gated; Open311 retired; Dynamics API internal.
notes.openData: The flagship — 311 Service Requests (erm2-nwe9): 1.26M views, 590k downloads.
notes.coreData: Service requests fully machine-readable; live SODA over tens of millions of records.
notes.writeApi: Open311 POST /requests retired; no public submit/track API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: The entire citizen service runs on a Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.

notes.platform: Legacy aNNN-*.nyc.gov app layer (BIS/DOB NOW) behind Akamai.
notes.siteApi: BIS Web / DOB NOW are browser-only; no public API.
notes.openData: 44 datasets — but a nightly one-way batch dump (DOBRunDate).
notes.coreData: Permits/violations are in the batch dump, one-way.
notes.writeApi: Filing a permit has no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Owned transactional core, not a vendor.

notes.platform: Angular SPA over a private owned REST API (WSO2).
notes.siteApi: A modern, versioned, owned REST API exists — but kept private.
notes.openData: 47 flattened snapshots.
notes.coreData: A real API backs HPD Online.
notes.writeApi: Housing Connect lottery is a closed silo, no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: HPD owns its API outright.

notes.platform: Legacy .shtml on nyc.gov + Drupal (nycstreetdesign.info).
notes.siteApi: NYCSTREET permitting portal has no API.
notes.openData: 267 assets — the most of any domain.
notes.coreData: Scattered flat snapshots + map-only visualizations keyed to a street SegmentID.
notes.writeApi: Street-work permit has no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Owned data, no productized surface.

notes.platform: NYC.gov CMS over Accela COTS + ASP.NET apps.
notes.siteApi: Accela back-office apps; no open API.
notes.openData: 81 datasets incl. the famous restaurant inspections.
notes.coreData: Inspections open; live grades/permits siloed.
notes.writeApi: Ordering a birth/death certificate has no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Transactions run on Accela COTS (vendor).

notes.platform: Oracle WebCenter Sites + React forms over ASP.NET.
notes.siteApi: Live first-party collection-schedule + pickup APIs, undocumented.
notes.openData: 51 datasets.
notes.coreData: Schedules/tonnage open; the live backends aren't public.
notes.writeApi: Bulk pickup scheduling has no public API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: NYC-owned backends, just undocumented.

notes.platform: Oracle WebCenter Sites + Angular SPAs (nypdonline, CompStat).
notes.siteApi: Live surfaces trapped in Angular apps on an undocumented Azure Gov backend.
notes.openData: 42 datasets incl. the portal's most-viewed (collisions).
notes.coreData: Flattened snapshots.
notes.writeApi: Report/FOIL request has no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Owned but undocumented.

notes.platform: NYC.gov CMS; data on Socrata + a CloudFront/S3 parquet host.
notes.siteApi: No API; trip records are bulk parquet files.
notes.openData: 80 datasets + the famous trip-record parquet dumps.
notes.coreData: Batch parquet + flat snapshots, not queryable.
notes.writeApi: License application has no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Owned data, no live surface.

notes.platform: Modern Planning Labs apps (ZoLa) + an open-source geocoder.
notes.siteApi: GeoClient/Geosupport on Azure APIM, subscription-gated.
notes.openData: 188 datasets incl. PLUTO.
notes.coreData: The city's geography — machine-readable and authoritative.
notes.writeApi: Reference agency — no citizen write-workflow (ULURP is agency/applicant).
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Owns the geocoder; GeoClient is gated. The natural nyc-commons anchor.

notes.platform: WordPress (office) + Drupal (Checkbook NYC), behind Imperva.
notes.siteApi: Checkbook NYC is a live keyless public API — but XML-envelope, POST-only, no OpenAPI/JSON/MCP.
notes.openData: 15 assets (top one federates to Checkbook).
notes.coreData: Checkbook holds the full spending/contract/payroll record.
notes.writeApi: eClaim is a web form, no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Owns Checkbook, but siloed from Open Data + eClaim.

notes.platform: NYC.gov Livesite + Oracle Siebel CRM Self-Service portal.
notes.siteApi: Siebel CRM; no API.
notes.openData: 24 datasets (reference data).
notes.coreData: Developments/reference open; resident service data closed.
notes.writeApi: Reporting a repair work order has no API.
notes.agent: None.
notes.ownership: Resident transactions locked in a vendor Siebel CRM.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace); no ACS-specific application at all.
notes.siteApi: No ACS content or site API; informational HTML only.
notes.openData: 21 assets but 16 are non-tabular file attachments; only 5 tabular and just 1 address-level.
notes.coreData: Operational data confidential by statute — published only as aggregate reports, mostly as files, never per case.
notes.writeApi: No ACS-owned write surface; abuse reports go to the NY State Central Register, provider complaints to NYC 311.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no structured intake, only downloadable spreadsheets and delegated web forms.
notes.ownership: ACS owns the Community Partners directory but delegates both public intakes; ownership of the transaction layer is absent.

notes.platform: Two managed platforms — NYC.gov Livesite + Salesforce Experience Cloud — modern but the transaction layer is a rented SaaS.
notes.siteApi: No content or portal API; the informational site and Salesforce portal expose no machine-readable surface.
notes.openData: 9 rich datasets — licensees, three registrant streams, markets, fleet, violations, complaints, denials — high traffic.
notes.coreData: The core object (licensee/registration) is fully published as an open registry keyed on BIC NUMBER.
notes.writeApi: No write API — apply, renew, and pay-a-fine all locked in the Salesforce portal or on paper.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; the OpenAPI + MCP artifact here are the proposal.
notes.ownership: Open data is city-owned, but the licensing system-of-record and its transactions are locked in a vendor SaaS with no export/API.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; no BSA-specific platform; intake is paper PDF forms.
notes.siteApi: No site/records API — resolutions search is a server-rendered Livesite widget, no JSON/OpenAPI.
notes.openData: 4 Socrata datasets covering case status, decisions map, legacy index, pre-app meetings; no filing/hearing data.
notes.coreData: Core Application/case object well modeled in Open Data; resolutions only as PDF links; no hearing calendar.
notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — filing a variance/appeal is a download-a-PDF-and-file-on-paper process.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP; search is not machine-callable.
notes.ownership: Case data on Socrata/Tyler; site on shared Livesite; only the PDF forms are BSA's own — mixed, vendor-hosted.

notes.platform: Modern managed NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) but shared and content-only.
notes.siteApi: No machine-readable site/content API; the intake is an HTML form, not an application.
notes.openData: Only 3 thin, aggregate operational datasets on Socrata SODA; no law, guidance, or case data.
notes.coreData: Core entities (complaints, cases, protected classes, guidance) exist as none-structured — form fields, prose, and PDF.
notes.writeApi: Report Discrimination is an untyped HTML form posting to an opaque backend; no write API.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind; nothing an agent can call.
notes.ownership: Runs on the city's own platform with no vendor CRM lock-in; but the case backend is opaque and the intake is untyped.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) + a standalone status-lookup app; modern managed hosting but generic and API-less.
notes.siteApi: No content or site API; the informational site and DTI dashboards expose HTML/JS only.
notes.openData: National model — 4 daily-updated, automated, disaggregated Socrata datasets plus interactive DTI dashboards.
notes.coreData: Core objects (Complaint, Allegation, PoliceOfficer, Penalty) richly modeled and officer-level; CCRB-vs-NYPD disposition and penalty gaps preserved.
notes.writeApi: Filing a complaint exists as an online web form + 311 + mail, but there is no intake API and status is a disconnected screen.
notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface, no queryable API over the open data.
notes.ownership: CCRB owns and actively drives its own transparency (DTI) and the intake/status apps, but rides a citywide shared platform and exposes no API of its own.

notes.platform: Own domain, coherent stack, but legacy IIS 8.5 / ASP.NET MVC 5.2 / AngularJS 1.5 and ASP.NET WebForms filing apps.
notes.siteApi: A real, working JSON Web API (FTMSearchWebAPI) exists — but undocumented, backend-only, no OpenAPI or contract.
notes.openData: 16 Socrata datasets + bulk per-cycle CSV Data Library (2001-2025) + public dashboards; among the most open in NYC.
notes.coreData: Contributions (with bundler detail), expenditures, public funds, and per-candidate financial analysis all richly published.
notes.writeApi: Filing a disclosure statement is only possible inside the login-walled C-SMART app; no submission API, no Open Data twin.
notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface, no documented API for an agent to call.
notes.ownership: CFB owns its domain, its search app, its data library, and its API — very high self-ownership, unlike rented or hidden stacks.

notes.platform: modern in places (Azure/.NET City Jobs, Shopify store) but fragmented across rented aNNN apps + legacy PeopleSoft
notes.siteApi: nyc.gov/site/dcas is Livesite content only — no content API
notes.openData: 32 datasets, civil-service + buildings + fleet, some with millions of views
notes.coreData: titles, eligible lists, exam schedule, postings, buildings, fleet all published and richly columned
notes.writeApi: no write API; exam registration and job application locked in OASys / City Jobs
notes.agent: no MCP or agent-native surface anywhere
notes.ownership: data on rented Socrata; every transaction app is a vendor tenant (Azure, PeopleSoft, Shopify)

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite CMS + a modern Salesforce Experience Cloud grants portal — maintained managed platforms, but not API-first.
notes.siteApi: No content API; the grants portal is a login-gated Salesforce community with no documented public API.
notes.openData: Good but thin — 9 datasets publish funding outcomes, the org directory, public art, and MFTA, but only the outcome side of the lifecycle.
notes.coreData: Core entities present, but funding datasets key on organization name + application # with no stable org id, so joins are messy.
notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere — applying for a grant goes only through the Salesforce Grants Management System.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface today; no MCP, no OpenAPI, no schemas published by the agency.
notes.ownership: Owns and maintains its open datasets, but the grant-application transaction runs on a vendor Salesforce org it configures rather than controls.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite CMS + a modern Java NYC Business portal + CityPay — maintained but not API-first.
notes.siteApi: No content API; the licensing portal is a session-gated Java app with no documented public API.
notes.openData: Exceptional — 37 datasets publish the full licensing/enforcement lifecycle, incl. Issued Licenses (147k views).
notes.coreData: Core entities richly modeled and joinable on Business Unique ID / License Number across the lifecycle.
notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere — apply for a license and file a complaint both go through a portal or 311.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface today; no MCP, no OpenAPI, no schemas published by the agency.
notes.ownership: Owns and actively maintains its open datasets, but the transaction layer runs on a shared NYC Business portal it does not solely control.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; no DDC-owned application platform with an API, and the transaction layer is citywide systems (PASSPort/MOCS, City Record, Checkbook).
notes.siteApi: No site or content API; nothing machine-readable on nyc.gov/site/ddc.
notes.openData: Only 4 Socrata datasets, three of them frozen '(Historical)' snapshots and one a 4-column contract directory — present but thin and stale.
notes.coreData: CapitalProject and AwardedContract are modeled and open but only as snapshots; vendor and division are derived; no live status.
notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — solicitation, prequalification, and onboarding are citywide PASSPort/MOCS; no citizen write exists.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no structured intake an agent could call.
notes.ownership: DDC owns almost none of its surface — reference data is thin and partly published under OMB/Comptroller, and the transaction systems belong to MOCS and the Comptroller.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite site + modern-but-packaged uMAX CIS (Azure B2C)
notes.siteApi: No content or site API; informational HTML only
notes.openData: 57 Socrata datasets, broad coverage, well-trafficked
notes.coreData: Open but sprawling/inconsistent — 100-col text tables, cryptic SCADA tags, no clean model
notes.writeApi: None — bill pay/account in uMAX portal; service requests go to generic 311
notes.agent: No agent-native surface at all
notes.ownership: Data via rented Socrata; transactions in rented uMAX + citywide 311

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; no DFTA-owned application platform, service layer is a phone contact center.
notes.siteApi: No site or content API; nothing machine-readable on nyc.gov/site/dfta.
notes.openData: 11 open Socrata datasets covering the provider network well, but no service-layer data.
notes.coreData: Core provider/center/activity/service-unit entities are modeled and open; participation is aggregate-only.
notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — every referral/intake is a phone call to Aging Connect.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no structured intake an agent could call.
notes.ownership: Reference data is DFTA-labeled on Open Data, but the transaction layer is an unowned contact-center process.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' CMS (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace); no DHS-specific or modern platform
notes.siteApi: Informational site only; no content API
notes.openData: 23 datasets led by the flagship DHS Daily Report (87k views); strong census/directory/facility coverage
notes.coreData: Core entities well modeled but aggregate-only snapshots; no live per-shelter roster or individual records
notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere; every action is a 311 call, 911, or in-person intake
notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind
notes.ownership: Reference data lives on NYC's Socrata tenant; the stand-in service channel (NYC311) belongs to another agency, not DHS

notes.platform: Standard NYC.gov Livesite chassis plus a legacy Apache MyFaces (JSF) lookup on a WebSphere-era stack
notes.siteApi: No content or site API; the informational site is HTML only
notes.openData: 15 Socrata datasets — unusually rich accountability data (custody, admissions, deaths, incidents, security indicators)
notes.coreData: Core person-in-custody data is published daily and searchable live, but only via a JSF screen and daily snapshots — no per-person API
notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — visits, complaints, and records requests are phone/mail/in-person or vendor portals
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; the live lookup is a JavaScript/JSF app hostile to automation
notes.ownership: First-party datasets and a city-hosted lookup (a073.nyc.gov), not a rented SaaS — reasonably owned, but the lookup is undocumented legacy

notes.platform: NYC.gov Livesite chassis + a fleet of legacy a836 apps (ACRIS ASP.NET/IIS 2013, CityPay, PTS) — dated but in-house.
notes.siteApi: No content or site API; ACRIS/CityPay/PTS are server-rendered apps, not APIs — no OpenAPI, no JSON.
notes.openData: Widest footprint assessed — 145 DOF datasets across valuation, ACRIS, exemptions, tax charges, and parking.
notes.coreData: Every core entity (valuation, exemptions, tax-charge balance, ACRIS documents, parking violations) has a rich, machine-readable Open Data twin.
notes.writeApi: No payment/transaction API — paying a ticket or tax bill exists only inside the CityPay/PTS web apps.
notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface; the legacy apps are browser-only.
notes.ownership: DOF owns its systems and data in-house (ACRIS, CityPay, PTS built for the city; data on Socrata) — not rented to a SaaS vendor.

notes.platform: Modern shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis for the info site, but no DOI-owned application; the complaint service layer is a third-party vendor SaaS (Kaseware).
notes.siteApi: No content API; the site is HTML and the reports are static PDFs.
notes.openData: 4 datasets — genuinely useful (Evictions is a ~237k-view asset) but mostly City Marshal oversight, not DOI's investigations.
notes.coreData: The core object — a public investigation report — is published only as PDF, never structured; PPR gives a partial structured recommendation twin.
notes.writeApi: None — filing a corruption complaint is a Kaseware vendor form plus phone/fax/mail.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no clean OpenAPI, no structured report or complaint contract.
notes.ownership: DOI owns the marshal/eviction and PPR data (open), but its investigative reports are PDFs and its complaint intake is leased to a third-party vendor.

notes.platform: Fragmented across NYC.gov Livesite, a Squarespace site, a Preservica DAMS, a deprecated LUNA, and two Akamai portals — no coherent platform.
notes.siteApi: Informational/collections sites are HTML only; the DAMS has a REST content API but it is token-gated with no OpenAPI.
notes.openData: 13 rich index/finding-aid datasets (vital records, archives, publications, honorary streets), but they index rather than deliver, and the top dataset is a delimited blob.
notes.coreData: The core objects — the scans, photographs, films, certificates — live only in the vendor DAMS; Open Data carries indexes, never the asset.
notes.writeApi: None — ordering a record, requesting a reproduction, and filing a FOIL are portal/form/email only.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no clean OpenAPI, no resolvable object references.
notes.ownership: DORIS owns the indexes (open data) but leases the archive of record to Preservica and routes retrieval through Akamai apps it does not expose.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; no DVS-owned application platform, the service layer is a third-party vendor portal (Combined Arms / VetConnectNYC).
notes.siteApi: No site or content API; nothing machine-readable on nyc.gov/site/veterans.
notes.openData: 7 open Socrata datasets — unusually deep for a small agency, reaching into de-identified service data, but modest in count and partly historical.
notes.coreData: Core resource/business/assistance-request/case entities are modeled and open; client data is aggregate/de-identified only.
notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — every referral is a Combined Arms web form worked manually within 3-5 business days.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no structured intake an agent could call.
notes.ownership: Reference and de-identified service data are DVS-labeled and open, but the live transaction layer runs on an out-of-city third-party vendor DVS does not own.

notes.platform: Informational site on the shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; DYCD Connect / DiscoverDYCD is a real, custom, DYCD-owned Angular-on-IIS application
notes.siteApi: DiscoverDYCD has an internal /api/ backend, but it is undocumented, private, and returns 404 to any public probe — no documented site API
notes.openData: 15 datasets, strong on the supply side (program sites 34c, contracts, providers, aggregate demographics 52c) but no clean catalog of the program offerings themselves
notes.coreData: Program sites, providers, and contracts are modeled with keys and a full geography spine; 'Program' as an offering lives only inside the finder app
notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere — applying to a program (SYEP, COMPASS) has no public, machine-readable surface
notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native contract; the finder is a human-only Angular UI
notes.ownership: DYCD owns and operates its own finder and portal (unlike rented search); open data is owned via Socrata

notes.platform: informational site on shared NYC.gov Livesite; the transaction platform is a rented Accela Civic Platform SaaS
notes.siteApi: nyc.gov/site/fdny is content-only — no content API
notes.openData: 17 datasets, generous — firehouses, minute-level fire & EMS dispatch, inspections, violations, certificates
notes.coreData: reference & incident data strong, but live permit / C of F / inspection status is only historical snapshots; current status is behind Accela
notes.writeApi: no write API; permits, C of F, and inspection requests are Accela UI / paper only
notes.agent: no MCP or agent-native surface exists today
notes.ownership: incident-data pipeline is owned; the business transaction system of record is a rented commercial SaaS (Accela)

notes.platform: Informational site on shared NYC.gov Livesite; ACCESS NYC is a modern open-source WordPress app and ACCESS HRA a React SPA — modern but split across three stacks.
notes.siteApi: No content/site API; the ACCESS NYC screener returns HTML and the benefits catalog is reachable only as an Open Data export.
notes.openData: 49 datasets — SNAP/Cash Assistance/Medicaid caseloads, application-center directories, and 75-column case-action reports.
notes.coreData: Rich aggregates and directories, but no individual application/case entity is ever published (aggregate case actions are the closest twin).
notes.writeApi: No public API for the core transaction — applying for benefits lives only in the ACCESS HRA React portal or an in-person visit.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; neither the screener nor the application portal exposes tools or a machine-readable contract.
notes.ownership: Eligibility rules are open-sourced and city-owned (rare) and the application portal is a city-built React app, but there is no owned API binding them.

notes.platform: Modern-ish NYC.gov Content API v2 SPA, but client-side jQuery/Bootstrap; migrated off its own legacy .ny.us domain.
notes.siteApi: A real public JSON content API exists (apps.nyc.gov/content-api/v2) but is undocumented, unversioned, and not offered as a consumer contract.
notes.openData: 20 machine-readable Socrata datasets, but WIDE/pivoted, historical, and awkward to consume; agency label is 'NYC ...(IBO)'.
notes.coreData: IBO's core product (~1,145 publications) is well-published via the content API, but the underlying analytical data (forecasts, models) stays in PDFs/interactives.
notes.writeApi: None — Ask IBO is a form/email; IBO has no transactional API surface at all.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface: no MCP, no OpenAPI, undocumented content API, search rented to Google.
notes.ownership: First-party over its publications and datasets on shared NYC.gov infra, but data-viz and search outsourced to Airtable/ArcGIS/Infogram/Google.

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; nothing Law-specific to point at.
notes.siteApi: No content or site API of any kind.
notes.openData: 7 datasets, all 'No' automation / annual — present but thin and stale.
notes.coreData: Only a case index is Law's; the core claims/settlement ledger belongs to the Comptroller.
notes.writeApi: No write surface at all; applications handled by email/PDF.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface exists today.
notes.ownership: Data on Socrata, litigation money data owned by another agency, site on a shared chassis.

notes.platform: Three vendor platforms (Livesite, Esri ArcGIS Online, Salesforce Experience Cloud), none an owned LPC API surface.
notes.siteApi: No content API on the info site; the Discover map is backed by ArcGIS Online feature services, queryable but not documented or owned as an LPC API.
notes.openData: 15 Socrata datasets — landmarks, districts, building database, permit history, complaints, violations, grants, archaeology — richly published.
notes.coreData: Core designation entities well modeled and consistently keyed on LP_NUMBER and BBL/BIN; building database carries architect/style/materials/dates.
notes.writeApi: Filing a permit (Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect) has no API — it lives only in the Salesforce Portico portal.
notes.agent: No MCP or agent-native surface today; this assessment proposes the first.
notes.ownership: LPC publishes open data but owns no API; read is spread across Socrata + Esri and the write transaction is a Salesforce community.

notes.platform: Managed WordPress (WP Engine) + Cloudflare + Kadence/ACF — a competent marketing CMS, wrong tool for a data/coordination mission.
notes.siteApi: Accidental but functional WordPress wp/v2 REST API exposes programs/reports/solicitations as JSON; undocumented, unintended, not a product.
notes.openData: Exactly one dataset (Supervised Release Dockets, 6 cols), unchanged since 2023; near-absence.
notes.coreData: Core criminal-justice metrics live in PDF explainers and other agencies' dashboards, not as data.
notes.writeApi: Only generic Contact Form 7 web forms (vendor enrollment, contact); referrals and procurement responses happen offline.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; the MCP artifact here is entirely net-new.
notes.ownership: A convening office: depends on DOC/NYPD/DCJS data and a vendor CMS host; owns little of its own data.

notes.platform: informational site on shared NYC.gov Livesite; the flagship alerting platform is a rented Everbridge SaaS
notes.siteApi: nyc.gov/site/em is content-only — no content API
notes.openData: 13 datasets — solid on incidents, evacuation zones/centers, notifications archive, hazard mitigation, CERT/Ready NY — but several are duplicate hazard-mitigation variants and maps
notes.coreData: the core mission data (real-time alerts) IS machine-readable via the CAP feed and evacuation zones are geospatial, but live cooling-center / shelter activation and the notifications archive are snapshots, not a live status API
notes.writeApi: no write API; Notify NYC enrollment is Everbridge Member Portal / SMS keyword / 311 only
notes.agent: no MCP or agent-native surface exists today
notes.ownership: the alert feed and subscription are rented from Everbridge; the open-data pipeline is owned

notes.platform: shared NYC.gov Livesite CMS + a dated Apache Struts / Oracle WebLogic transaction portal
notes.siteApi: no content or site API; HTML only
notes.openData: only 2 datasets, but the flagship Hearings Division Case Status is huge and updated daily
notes.coreData: case-status data is exceptionally rich and machine-readable — every summons, charge, hearing, decision, penalty, and balance
notes.writeApi: responding to / disputing a summons has no API — Struts portal, mail, or in person only
notes.agent: no agent-native surface of any kind
notes.ownership: data on Tyler/Socrata; the respond-to-summons transaction on a vendor Struts/WebLogic stack

notes.platform: Modern shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) — but only an informational site; no OCME-owned application.
notes.siteApi: No content API; the site is HTML only.
notes.openData: Exactly one OCME-labeled dataset, and it is a stale 2015-16 MMR performance-indicator passthrough, not casework.
notes.coreData: The core object (a death investigation) is never published — not per-decedent, not in aggregate; no responsible casework surface exists today.
notes.writeApi: No write API and no application — records requests are notarized paper forms plus NYC 311.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind.
notes.ownership: OCME owns almost none of its digital footprint — rides NYC.gov, NYC 311, and federal NamUs; death certificates belong to DOHMH.

notes.platform: Operates the city's platforms (Socrata, Azure APIM, Livesite) — it IS the platform layer.
notes.siteApi: The OTI site is HTML with no content API, but OTI runs a real API gateway (api.nyc.gov).
notes.openData: 221 datasets incl. the catalog of itself and the city's most-viewed dataset (311).
notes.coreData: Core entities all open — LinkNYC, broadband, Wi-Fi, 311, planimetric base map.
notes.writeApi: No public write API; dataset publishing and gateway keys are internal/portal-only.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface today; MCP proposed here.
notes.ownership: Operates the stack but on vendor platforms (Socrata/Tyler + Microsoft Azure APIM).

notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite site plus a real, stateful MyCity Business portal (Spring + Adobe AEM) — modern-ish but session-bound and vendor-authored.
notes.siteApi: A real transactional portal exists, but it exposes no API, OpenAPI, or JSON — only a session-walled UI.
notes.openData: 28 rich SBS datasets on Socrata SODA (certified list 56c, BID trends 64c) — broad and well-columned.
notes.coreData: Reference/directory objects are open, but the core transactions (certification application, enrollment, licensing guidance) have no data twin — only the approved output.
notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere; certification, enrollment, and the Step-by-Step wizard are portal-only.
notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no machine-readable guidance/eligibility.
notes.ownership: Data lives on NYC's own Socrata; the portal is a city-run MyCity app (not a pure vendor CRM) but leans on Adobe AEM and session state.

netNew.object: PermitApplication
EnrollmentApplication
TestimonyRegistration
BallotRequest
ServiceRequest (Open311)
PermitApplication
HousingLotteryApplication
StreetWorkPermit
VitalRecordRequest
BulkPickupRequest
PoliceReportRequest
LicenseApplication
ClaimFiling
WorkOrder
ChildCareComplaint — submit a complaint about a child care provider (models the provider concern, never case data; abuse routed to the State Central Register)
File a consumer complaint (ConsumerComplaint) and apply for a business license (LicenseApplication) — the two citizen writes with no API
WaterServiceRequest

ServiceReferral — connect an older adult to DFTA services (case management, meals, benefits, caregiver, elder abuse, center enrollment) with an urgent path to Aging Connect / Adult Protective Services.
OutreachRequest — request street outreach for a person experiencing homelessness (today only via NYC311)
VisitScheduling — schedule a visit with a person in custody (no API today; arranged by phone, in person, or vendor portal). Complaint / records request is a second net-new write.
ParkingTicketPayment — pay a parking/camera violation via API instead of the CityPay-only web form.
ProgramApplication — apply to a program (e.g. an SYEP application)
FirePermitApplication — apply for and track a fire permit / Certificate of Operation / inspection request via FDNY Business
BenefitsApplication — apply for and track SNAP / Cash Assistance / Medicaid, today locked in the ACCESS HRA portal
ExamRegistration — register for a civil-service exam (alt: JobApplication)
Apply for a Cultural Development Fund grant (GrantApplication) — the core transaction with no API, locked in the Salesforce Grants Management System

VendorPrequalification — a firm submits a prequalification / expression of interest (optionally against a solicitation, with an optional City Record notice opt-in). A B2G write; DDC is vendor-facing, so there is no citizen write in this domain.
RecordsRequest — request/retrieve a record: order a copy of a historical vital record, an archival item/photo, a library publication, or file a FOIL request

ServiceReferral — make a VetConnectNYC care-coordination referral connecting a veteran to services (housing, benefits, VA claims, health, employment, education, food, legal), routed to DVS Care Coordinators with an urgent path to a Veteran Resource Center.
LandmarkPermitApplication — file a Certificate of Appropriateness / No Effect / Minor Work application (today Salesforce Portico only, no API)
NotifyNYCSubscription — subscribe to Notify NYC emergency alerts by channel, location, and category through an owned write API
DeathRecordRequest — request and track a decedent's medical-examiner casefile record/report
One owned, unified gateway + catalog API — plus two write surfaces: register a dataset, and request an api.nyc.gov gateway key.
MWBECertificationApplication — apply for M/WBE (also EBE/LBE) certification; no API today, only the MyCity Business portal.
TradeWasteLicenseApplication — apply for a trade waste license/registration
VarianceApplication — file a zoning variance / special permit / extension / appeal (today a paper PDF-form process)
A typed DiscriminationComplaint intake API (report discrimination) — replacing the untyped Report Discrimination HTML form.
MisconductComplaint — file a police-misconduct complaint against an officer via API (today an online web form only)
DisclosureFiling — a filer submits a disclosure statement (today only via the login-walled C-SMART disclosure application)

CorruptionComplaint — file and track a fraud/waste/corruption tip (with anonymous filing and Whistleblower-Law protection), the transaction that starts every DOI investigation and today lives only in a vendor Kaseware form.
DataRequest — an API for the 'Ask IBO' data/analysis request, today a web form / email only
LawInternshipApplication — submit a legal internship / fellowship application (the one citizen-initiated transaction with this agency)
ProgramReferral — route a person or court case to a MOCJ-coordinated program
SummonsDispute — respond to / dispute a summons (request a hearing) via an owned API
